element.grid_ref: covers the full plan grid1-4 x gridF-gridA plus overhangs -- a single unbroken rectangle, no notch/cutout
element.description: หลังคาเหล็กรีดลอน (corrugated metal sheet roofing), hatched vertically over the whole roofed plan; two 'SLOPE 6°' labels printed vertically near grid2 and grid3, indicating fall direction/pitch of approximately 6 degrees on each side of an internal fold line. Two curved swirl-arrow symbols near mid-plan (roughly gridC-gridD), likely roof drainage/water-flow direction markers, not confidently classified further.
element.ridge_or_valley_lines: one internal fold line runs roughly north-south between the two 'SLOPE 6°' labels (near grid2-grid3) -- not confidently classified as a true ridge vs. valley at this resolution

near grid4, hatched vertical strip spanning gridD-gridC(approx), level +6.65

warnings: This sheet (A-06) is the roof plan for the whole 2-story building -- since the roof sits above the 2nd floor, its footprint corresponds to the 2nd-floor plan (หน้า07/A-05), not the ground floor.

This house's roof is a single unbroken rectangle over the full grid1-4 x gridF-gridA footprint plus overhangs -- no notch/cutout over the carport (unlike some sister houses), meaning the ground-floor จอดรถ (carport, see _หน้า06_floor_plan_ground.json) is fully roofed here, not open to the sky. Not independently confirmed against a structural roof-framing sheet (outside this extraction's architecture-only scope).
The 'SLOPE 6°' labels are printed vertically near grid2/grid3, matching the same 6-degree value and general convention seen on sister houses' roof plans.

Overhang dimensions (1.30m east/west, 2.00m north, 1.50m south) are independently corroborated by the top/left dimension chains on the floor plan sheets (หน้า06/07) which show matching 0.60/0.50 west/east sub-splits at the building-line edge; the larger 2.00m/1.50m north/south overhangs here (vs. the floor plans' 1.00m north / 2.05m ST1-related south dimension) reflect the roof eave projecting further out than the floor slab edge, as expected.

Three separate 'กันสาด ค.ส.ล.' (concrete canopy) callouts are printed at roof level (+6.65 west, +6.65 east, +6.55 south) -- distinct from the two 2nd-floor-level canopies (+3.65) already recorded on the upper floor plan (หน้า07, 'หลังคา ค.ส.ล' near ห้องน้ำ3). The two hatched vertical strips near grid1 and grid4 are the west/east roof-level canopies; the south one (+6.55, 0.10m lower, not hatched in the same way) sits below grid A near the entrance/ST1 area.

Actual roof framing (purlins/rafters/trusses) is not detailed on this sheet -- references to structural engineering drawings would be expected (outside this extraction's architecture-only page range 03-28), consistent with the section sheets (หน้า13-14, A-11/A-12) noting 'โครงหลังคา ดูแบบวิศวกรรมโครงสร้าง'.

No dummy-grid-relevant feature is visible on this roof plan south of grid A beyond the +6.55 canopy note -- the roof plan does not extend down to show the ST1 entrance-stair wedge found on the ground floor plan (หน้า06) at 2.05m south of grid A, consistent with that being a small ground-level feature not requiring its own roof coverage beyond the general eave overhang.
